He held a [[Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in {{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部}}. During [[World War II]], he was conscripted into the [[Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Recipients of the Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] {{Otheruses|archaeologist|bureaucrat|Kobayashi Yukio (bureaucrat)}} {{Image request|face photo|date=January 2021|cat=People}} {{Infobox scholar |name = Yukio Kobayashi
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |image = |image_size = |alt = |caption = |full_name = |other_names = |birth_name = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1911|08|18|no}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}・{{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} |death_date = {{Death date and age|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |death_place = |death_cause = |residence = |citizenship = |nationality = {{JPN}} |alma_mater = {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校}}
(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[1911]] – February 2, [[1989]]) was a Japanese [[archaeologist]] and [[Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in {{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部}}. During [[World War II]], he was conscripted into the [[Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Recipients of the Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] revision.text:
{{Otheruses|archaeologist|bureaucrat|Kobayashi Yukio (bureaucrat)}} {{Image request|face photo|date=January 2021|cat=People}} {{Infobox scholar |name = Yukio Kobayashi
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |image = |image_size = |alt = |caption = |full_name = |other_names = |birth_name = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1911|08|18|no}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}・{{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} |death_date = {{Death date and age|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |death_place = |death_cause = |residence = |citizenship = |nationality = {{JPN}} |alma_mater = {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校}}
(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[1911]] – February 2, [[1989]]) was a Japanese [[archaeologist]] and [[Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in {{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部}}. During [[World War II]], he was conscripted into the [[Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Recipients of the Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] [[Category:Kyoto University faculty]] [[Category:Kobe University alumni]] [[Category:People from Kobe]] [[Category:1911 births]] [[Category:1989 deaths]] [[Category:Peripheral articles]] [[category:Kobayashi Yukio]][[Category:11]][[category:Kobayashi Yukio]][[Category:11]] [[category:Kobayashi Yukio]][[category:Kobayashi Yukio]][[category:Kobayashi Yukio]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Kobayashi Yukio]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Kobayashi Yukio]][[Category:11]] [[ja:小林行雄]] [[Category:ginality]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kobayashi Yukio]][[Category:11]] (now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[1911]] – February 2, [[1989]]) was a Japanese [[archaeologist]] and [[Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in {{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部}}. During [[World War II]], he was conscripted into the [[Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Recipients of the Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|12=draft}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|12=draft}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|12=draft}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * {{Otheruses|archaeologist|bureaucrat|Kobayashi Yukio (bureaucrat)}} {{Image request|face photo|date=January 2021|cat=People}} {{Infobox scholar |name = Yukio Kobayashi
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |image = |image_size = |alt = |caption = |full_name = |other_names = |birth_name = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1911|08|18|no}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}・{{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}} |death_date = {{Death date and age|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |death_place = |death_cause = |residence = |citizenship = |nationality = {{JPN}} |alma_mater = {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|12=draft}}
(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|12=draft}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授|12=draft}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[1911]] – February 2, [[1989]]) was a Japanese [[archaeologist]] and [[Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in {{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|12=draft}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|12=draft}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|12=draft}}. During [[World War II]], he was conscripted into the [[Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|12=draft}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|12=draft}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|12=draft}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|12=draft}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|12=draft}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|12=draft}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] He held a [[Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in {{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|12=draft}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|12=draft}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|12=draft}}. During [[World War II]], he was conscripted into the [[Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|12=draft}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|12=draft}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|12=draft}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|12=draft}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|12=draft}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|12=draft}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kobayashi Yukio]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Recipients of the Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] [[Category:Kyoto University faculty]] [[Category:Kobe University alumni]] [[Category:People from Kobe]] [[Category:1911 births]] [[Category:1989 deaths]] [[Category:Peripheral articles]] revision.comment: 42 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:小林行雄 up to rev 98418913
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|考古学者|文部官僚|小林行雄 (文部官僚)}} {{画像提供依頼|顔写真|date=2021年1月|cat=人物}} {{Infobox 学者 |名前 = 小林 行雄
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |画像 = |画像サイズ = |画像代替説明 = |画像説明 = |全名 = |別名 = |誕生名 = |生年月日 = {{生年月日と年齢|1911|08|18|no}} |生誕地 = {{JPN}}・[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]] |没年月日 = {{死亡年月日と没年齢|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |死没地 = |死因 = |居住 = |市民権 = |国籍 = {{JPN}} |出身校 = [[神戸高等工業学校]]
（現：[[神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|神戸大学工学部]]） |配偶者 = |両親 = |子供 = |時代 = |活動地域 = [[日本]] |学派 = |研究分野 = [[考古学]] |研究機関 = [[京都大学]] |博士課程指導教員 = |他の指導教員 = |博士課程指導学生 = |主な指導学生 = |学位 = [[博士（文学）|文学博士]] |称号 = [[京都大学]][[名誉教授]] |特筆すべき概念 = |主な業績 = |主要な作品 = |影響を受けた人物 = |影響を与えた人物 = |学会 = |主な受賞歴 = [[恩賜賞 (日本学士院)|日本学士院恩賜賞]] |署名 = |公式サイト = |脚注 = }} '''小林 行雄'''（こばやし ゆきお、[[1911年]]（[[明治]]44年）[[8月18日]] - [[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）[[2月2日]]）は、[[日本]]の[[考古学者]]。[[京都大学]]名誉教授。学位は、[[博士（文学）|文学博士]]。[[恩賜賞 (日本学士院)|日本学士院恩賜賞]]受賞者。 == 来歴 == [[兵庫県]][[神戸市]]に生まれる。1932年（昭和7年）、[[神戸高等工業学校]]（現：[[神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|神戸大学工学部]]）を卒業[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 『官報』第1593号、昭和7年4月23日、p.703]し、副手に就任。後に依願退職し、近畿地方を中心に発掘調査に携わる。1935年（昭和10年）、[[京都大学#沿革|京都帝国大学]][[京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|文学部]]助手。1945年（昭和20年）に[[大日本帝国海軍|海軍]]に招集され、戦後は1953年（昭和28年）に[[京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|京都大学文学部]][[講師　(教育)#高等教育|講師]]、1954（昭和29年）年には[[恩賜賞 (日本学士院)|日本学士院恩賜賞]]を受賞。1955年1月、『史林』38巻1号に「古墳発生の歴史的意義」を発表。1974年（昭和49年）、京都大学文学部[[教授]]に昇進し、1975年（昭和50年）に退官、[[名誉教授]]。 1937年（昭和12年）九州から[[近畿地方]]にまで分布する土器に共通した特徴があることに気づき、その土器に「[[遠賀川式土器|遠賀川式]]」と名付けた。 遺跡や古墳から出土する遺物に着目し、精緻な系統的分類を行う研究手法で知られる。この[[方法論]]は後の日本の[[古代社会]]の動向や文化理解のための基本的な方法論として確立させるという不朽不滅の業績を残したとされる。特に1965年（昭和40年）の論文書『古鏡』における[[三角縁神獣鏡]]の形式学的な遺物研究は大きく評価されている[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 小林行雄とは - コトバンク]。 == 著書 == * 『大和唐古弥生式遺跡の研究』桑名文星堂 1943 * 『日本古代文化の諸問題 考古学者の対話』高桐書院 1947 * 『日本考古学概説』創元選書 1951 * 『[[福岡県]][[糸島郡]]一貴山村田中銚子塚古墳の研究』[[便利堂]] 1952 * 『[[古墳]]の話』[[岩波書店]]、1959年 * 『[[陶器]]全集 第1巻 [[埴輪]]』[[平凡社]] 1960 * 『[[古墳時代]]の研究』[[青木書店]] 1961 * 『古代の技術』正続 塙選書 1962-1964 * 『古鏡』[[学生社]] 1965 * 『国民の歴史 女王国の出現』[[文英堂]] 1967 * 『民族の起源』塙新書 1972 * 『古墳文化論考』平凡社 1976 * 『'''小林行雄考古学選集'''』真陽社 :1、弥生文化の研究　2005年 :2、古墳文化の研究　2010年 ===共著・編 === * 『[[弥生土器|弥生式土器]]聚成図録』正編 東京考古学会、1938-1939年、[[森本六爾]]と共同編集、解説は翌年。弥生文化が北九州に始まって東に波及する状況などが明らかになった。 *『弥生式土器集成』第1-2 [[杉原荘介]]共編 弥生式土器集成刊行会 1958-61 *『図解考古学辞典』[[水野清一]]共編 [[東京創元社]] 1959 *『世界考古学大系 第3巻 日本 第3（古墳時代）』編 [[平凡社]] 1959 *『世界考古学大系 第4巻 日本 第4（歴史時代）』[[浅野清 (建築学者)|浅野清]]共編 平凡社 1961 *『装飾古墳』編 [[藤本四八]]撮影 平凡社 1964 *『日本文学の歴史 第1巻 神と神を祭る者』[[池田弥三郎]]、[[角川源義]]共編 角川書店 1967 *『論集日本文化の起源 1 考古学』編 平凡社 1971 === 記念論文集 === *『考古学論考 小林行雄博士古稀記念論文集』平凡社 1982 == 関連図書 == * 『考古学論考 小林行雄博士古稀記念論文集』平凡社 1982 * 『考古学一路 小林行雄博士著作目録』平凡社 1983 * 『小林行雄先生追悼録』京都大学文学部考古学研究室 1994 * 『考古学京都学派』[[角田文衞|角田文衛]]編、雄山閣出版 増補版1997 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論]] {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こはやし ゆきお}} [[Category:日本の考古学者]] [[Category:日本学士院賞受賞者]] [[Category:文学博士取得者]] [[Category:京都大学の教員]] [[Category:神戸大学出身の人物]] [[Category:神戸市出身の人物]] [[Category:1911年生]] [[Category:1989年没]]

{{Otheruses|考古学者|文部官僚|小林行雄 (文部官僚)}} {{画像提供依頼|顔写真|date=2021年1月|cat=人物}} {{Infobox 学者 |名前 = 小林 行雄
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |画像 = |画像サイズ = |画像代替説明 = |画像説明 = |全名 = |別名 = |誕生名 = |生年月日 = {{生年月日と年齢|1911|08|18|no}} |生誕地 = {{JPN}}・[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]] |没年月日 = {{死亡年月日と没年齢|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |死没地 = |死因 = |居住 = |市民権 = |国籍 = {{JPN}} |出身校 = [[神戸高等工業学校]]
（現：[[神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|神戸大学工学部]]） |配偶者 = |両親 = |子供 = |時代 = |活動地域 = [[日本]] |学派 = |研究分野 = [[考古学]] |研究機関 = [[京都大学]] |博士課程指導教員 = |他の指導教員 = |博士課程指導学生 = |主な指導学生 = |学位 = [[博士（文学）|文学博士]] |称号 = [[京都大学]][[名誉教授]] |特筆すべき概念 = |主な業績 = |主要な作品 = |影響を受けた人物 = |影響を与えた人物 = |学会 = |主な受賞歴 = [[恩賜賞 (日本学士院)|日本学士院恩賜賞]] |署名 = |公式サイト = |脚注 = }} '''小林 行雄'''（こばやし ゆきお、[[1911年]]（[[明治]]44年）[[8月18日]] - [[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）[[2月2日]]）は、[[日本]]の[[考古学者]]。[[京都大学]]名誉教授。学位は、[[博士（文学）|文学博士]]。[[恩賜賞 (日本学士院)|日本学士院恩賜賞]]受賞者。 == 来歴 == [[兵庫県]][[神戸市]]に生まれる。1932年（昭和7年）、[[神戸高等工業学校]]（現：[[神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|神戸大学工学部]]）を卒業[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 『官報』第1593号、昭和7年4月23日、p.703]し、副手に就任。後に依願退職し、近畿地方を中心に発掘調査に携わる。1935年（昭和10年）、[[京都大学#沿革|京都帝国大学]][[京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|文学部]]助手。1945年（昭和20年）に[[大日本帝国海軍|海軍]]に招集され、戦後は1953年（昭和28年）に[[京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|京都大学文学部]][[講師　(教育)#高等教育|講師]]、1954（昭和29年）年には[[恩賜賞 (日本学士院)|日本学士院恩賜賞]]を受賞。1955年1月、『史林』38巻1号に「古墳発生の歴史的意義」を発表。1974年（昭和49年）、京都大学文学部[[教授]]に昇進し、1975年（昭和50年）に退官、[[名誉教授]]。 1937年（昭和12年）九州から[[近畿地方]]にまで分布する土器に共通した特徴があることに気づき、その土器に「[[遠賀川式土器|遠賀川式]]」と名付けた。 遺跡や古墳から出土する遺物に着目し、精緻な系統的分類を行う研究手法で知られる。この[[方法論]]は後の日本の[[古代社会]]の動向や文化理解のための基本的な方法論として確立させるという不朽不滅の業績を残したとされる。特に1965年（昭和40年）の論文書『古鏡』における[[三角縁神獣鏡]]の形式学的な遺物研究は大きく評価されている[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 小林行雄とは - コトバンク]。 == 著書 == * 『大和唐古弥生式遺跡の研究』桑名文星堂 1943 * 『日本古代文化の諸問題 考古学者の対話』高桐書院 1947 * 『日本考古学概説』創元選書 1951 * 『[[福岡県]][[糸島郡]]一貴山村田中銚子塚古墳の研究』[[便利堂]] 1952 * 『[[古墳]]の話』[[岩波書店]]、1959年 * 『[[陶器]]全集 第1巻 [[埴輪]]』[[平凡社]] 1960 * 『[[古墳時代]]の研究』[[青木書店]] 1961 * 『古代の技術』正続 塙選書 1962-1964 * 『古鏡』[[学生社]] 1965 * 『国民の歴史 女王国の出現』[[文英堂]] 1967 * 『民族の起源』塙新書 1972 * 『古墳文化論考』平凡社 1976 * 『'''小林行雄考古学選集'''』真陽社 :1、弥生文化の研究　2005年 :2、古墳文化の研究　2010年 ===共著・編 === * 『[[弥生土器|弥生式土器]]聚成図録』正編 東京考古学会、1938-1939年、[[森本六爾]]と共同編集、解説は翌年。弥生文化が北九州に始まって東に波及する状況などが明らかになった。 *『弥生式土器集成』第1-2 [[杉原荘介]]共編 弥生式土器集成刊行会 1958-61 *『図解考古学辞典』[[水野清一]]共編 [[東京創元社]] 1959 *『世界考古学大系 第3巻 日本 第3（古墳時代）』編 [[平凡社]] 1959 *『世界考古学大系 第4巻 日本 第4（歴史時代）』[[浅野清 (建築学者)|浅野清]]共編 平凡社 1961 *『装飾古墳』編 [[藤本四八]]撮影 平凡社 1964 *『日本文学の歴史 第1巻 神と神を祭る者』[[池田弥三郎]]、[[角川源義]]共編 角川書店 1967 *『論集日本文化の起源 1 考古学』編 平凡社 1971 === 記念論文集 === *『考古学論考 小林行雄博士古稀記念論文集』平凡社 1982 == 関連図書 == * 『考古学論考 小林行雄博士古稀記念論文集』平凡社 1982 * 『考古学一路 小林行雄博士著作目録』平凡社 1983 * 『小林行雄先生追悼録』京都大学文学部考古学研究室 1994 * 『考古学京都学派』[[角田文衞|角田文衛]]編、雄山閣出版 増補版1997 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論]] {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こはやし ゆきお}} [[Category:日本の考古学者]] [[Category:日本学士院賞受賞者]] [[Category:文学博士取得者]] [[Category:京都大学の教員]] [[Category:神戸大学出身の人物]] [[Category:神戸市出身の人物]] [[Category:1911年生]] [[Category:1989年没]]

{{Otheruses|archaeologist|bureaucrat|Kobayashi Yukio (bureaucrat)}} {{Image request|face photo|date=January 2021|cat=People}} {{Infobox scholar |name = Yukio Kobayashi
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |image = |image_size = |alt = |caption = |full_name = |other_names = |birth_name = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1911|08|18|no}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}・{{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} |death_date = {{Death date and age|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |death_place = |death_cause = |residence = |citizenship = |nationality = {{JPN}} |alma_mater = {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校}}
(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[1911]] – February 2, [[1989]]) was a Japanese [[archaeologist]] and [[Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in {{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University}}. During [[World War II]], he was conscripted into the [[Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kobayashi Yukio]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kobayashi Yukio]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Recipients of the Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] [[Category:Kyoto University faculty]] [[Category:Kobe University alumni]] [[Category:People from Kobe]] [[Category:1911 births]] [[Category:1989 deaths]] [[Category:Peripheral articles]] [[Category:Kobayashi Yukio]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kobayashi Yukio]] [[ja:小林行雄]] {{translated page|ja|小林行雄|version=98418913|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Otheruses|archaeologist|bureaucrat|Kobayashi Yukio (bureaucrat)}} {{Image request|face photo|date=January 2021|cat=People}} {{Infobox scholar |name = Yukio Kobayashi
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |image = |image_size = |alt = |caption = |full_name = |other_names = |birth_name = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1911|08|18|no}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}・{{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft}} |death_date = {{Death date and age|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |death_place = |death_cause = |residence = |citizenship = |nationality = {{JPN}} |alma_mater = {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|qq=draft}}
(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授|qq=draft}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[1911]] – February 2, [[1989]]) was a Japanese [[archaeologist]] and [[Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in {{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft}}. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|qq=draft}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=draft}}. During [[World War II]], he was conscripted into the [[Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|qq=draft}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|qq=draft}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|qq=draft}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|qq=draft}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|qq=draft}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|小林行雄|version=98418913|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:Recipients of the Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] [[Category:Kyoto University faculty]] [[Category:Kobe University alumni]] [[Category:People from Kobe]] [[Category:1911 births]] [[Category:1989 deaths]] [[Category:Peripheral articles]] [[Category:Kobayashi Yukio]] }} [[sn:Kobayashi Yukio]] [[ja:小林行雄]]

(1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected {{Otheruses|archaeologist|bureaucrat|Kobayashi Yukio (bureaucrat)}} {{Image request|face photo|date=January 2021|cat=People}} {{Infobox scholar |name = Yukio Kobayashi
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |image = |image_size = |alt = |caption = |full_name = |other_names = |birth_name = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1911|08|18|no}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}・{{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyogo Prefecture|qq=}} |death_date = {{Death date and age|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |death_place = |death_cause = |residence = |citizenship = |nationality = {{JPN}} |alma_mater = {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|qq=}}
(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professor Emeritus|qq=}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[1911]] – February 2, [[1989]]) was a Japanese [[archaeologist]] and [[Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in {{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyogo Prefecture|qq=}}. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|qq=}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=}}. During [[World War II]], he was conscripted into the [[Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|qq=}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|qq=}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|qq=}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|qq=}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|qq=}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|qq=}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated During [[World War II]], he was conscripted into the [[Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=}}. During [[World War II]], he was conscripted into the [[Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|qq=}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|qq=}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|qq=}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|qq=}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|qq=}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th (now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professor Emeritus|qq=}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[1911]] – February 2, [[1989]]) was a Japanese [[archaeologist]] and [[Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|qq=}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=}}. During [[World War II]], he was conscripted into the [[Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|qq=}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|qq=}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|qq=}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|qq=}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|qq=}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|qq=}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|小林行雄|version=98418913|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:Recipients of the Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] {{Otheruses|archaeologist|bureaucrat|Kobayashi Yukio (bureaucrat)}} {{Image request|face photo|date=January 2021|cat=People}} {{Infobox scholar |name = Yukio Kobayashi
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |image = |image_size = |alt = |caption = |full_name = |other_names = |birth_name = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1911|08|18|no}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}・[[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] |death_date = {{Death date and age|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |death_place = |death_cause = |residence = |citizenship = |nationality = {{JPN}} |alma_mater = {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|qq=}}
(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professor Emeritus|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[1911]] – February 2, [[1989]]) was a Japanese [[archaeologist]] and [[Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|qq=}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部}}. During [[World War II]], he was conscripted into the [[Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|qq=}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|qq=}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|qq=}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|qq=}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|qq=}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated {{Otheruses|archaeologist|bureaucrat|Kobayashi Yukio (bureaucrat)}} {{Image request|face photo|date=January 2021|cat=People}} {{Infobox scholar |name = Yukio Kobayashi
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |image = |image_size = |alt = |caption = |full_name = |other_names = |birth_name = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1911|08|18|no}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}・[[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] |death_date = {{Death date and age|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |death_place = |death_cause = |residence = |citizenship = |nationality = {{JPN}} |alma_mater = {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|qq=}}
(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professor Emeritus|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[:en:1911|1911]] – February 2, [[:en:1989|1989]]) was a Japanese [[archaeologist]] and [[Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|qq=}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部}}. During [[World War II]], he was conscripted into the [[Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|qq=}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|qq=}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|qq=}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|qq=}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * (now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professor Emeritus|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[:en:1911|1911]] – February 2, [[:en:1989|1989]]) was a Japanese [[archaeologist]] and [[Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|qq=}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部}}. During [[World War II]], he was conscripted into the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|qq=}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|qq=}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|qq=}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|qq=}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|qq=}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|小林行雄|version=98418913|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:1911 births]] [[Category:1989 deaths]] }} [[sn:Kobayashi Yukio]] *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen {{Otheruses|archaeologist|bureaucrat|Kobayashi Yukio (bureaucrat)}} {{Image request|face photo|date=January 2021|cat=People}} {{Infobox scholar |name = Yukio Kobayashi
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |image = |image_size = |alt = |caption = |full_name = |other_names = |birth_name = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1911|08|18|no}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}・[[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] |death_date = {{Death date and age|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |death_place = |death_cause = |residence = |citizenship = |nationality = {{JPN}} |alma_mater = {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|qq=}}
(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professor Emeritus|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[:en:Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[:en:1911|1911]] – February 2, [[:en:1989|1989]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Archaeologist|Archaeologist]] and [[Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[:en:Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|qq=}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部}}. During [[World War II]], he was conscripted into the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|qq=}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|qq=}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|qq=}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|qq=}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|qq=}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated
