(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professor Emeritus|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[:en:Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[:en:1911|1911]] – February 2, [[:en:1989|1989]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Archaeologist|Archaeologist]] and [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[:en:Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|qq=}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部}}. During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], he was conscripted into the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|qq=}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|qq=}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|qq=}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|qq=}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|qq=}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|小林行雄|version=98418913|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:1911 births]] [[Category:1989 deaths]] }} [[sn:Kobayashi Yukio]] In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部}}. During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], he was conscripted into the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|qq=}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|qq=}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|qq=}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|qq=}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|qq=}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|小林行雄|version=98418913|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:1911 births]] [[Category:1989 deaths]] }} [[sn:Kobayashi Yukio]] His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. He held a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[:en:Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|qq=|1=Kobe Higher Technical School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590260}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|1=Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56346162}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters, Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102104697}}. During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], he was conscripted into the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|qq=|1=Ongagawa-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642387}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|qq=|1=Rokuji Morimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539781}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|qq=|1=Sōsuke Sugihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218856}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|qq=|1=Seiichi Mizuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549425}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|qq=}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|小林行雄|version=98418913|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:1911 births]] [[Category:1989 deaths]] }} [[ja:小林行雄]] [[Category:Academic staff of Kyoto University]] [[Category:Wikipedia requested images of people]] [[Category:Doctor of literature holders]] [[Category:Laureates of Japan Academy Prize]] [[Category:Kobe University alumni]] [[Category:People from Kobe]] [[zh:小林行雄]] {{wikidata link|Q11461875}}

revision.text:
[[ja:小林行雄]][[zh:小林行雄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1911|8|18}}|death_date={{death date|1989|2|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}|awards={{ill|Imperial Prize of Japan Academy|WD=Q4389487}}|native_name=小林行雄|given_name={{ill|Yukio|WD=Q8060736}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11461875]] Wikidata name: Yukio Kobayashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11461875]] Wikidata name: Yukio Kobayashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|archaeologist|bureaucrat|Kobayashi Yukio (bureaucrat)}} {{Image request|face photo|date=January 2021|cat=People}} {{Infobox scholar |name = Yukio Kobayashi
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |image = |image_size = |alt = |caption = |full_name = |other_names = |birth_name = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1911|08|18|no}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}・[[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] |death_date = {{Death date and age|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |death_place = |death_cause = |residence = |citizenship = |nationality = {{JPN}} |alma_mater = {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|qq=|1=Kobe Higher Technical School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590260}}
(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|1=Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56346162}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professor Emeritus|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=professor emeritus|WD=Q1240569}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[:en:Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[:en:1911|1911]] – February 2, [[:en:1989|1989]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Archaeologist|Archaeologist]] and [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[:en:Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|qq=|1=Kobe Higher Technical School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590260}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|1=Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56346162}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters, Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102104697}}. During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], he was conscripted into the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|qq=|1=Ongagawa-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642387}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|qq=|1=Rokuji Morimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539781}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|qq=|1=Sōsuke Sugihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218856}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|qq=|1=Seiichi Mizuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549425}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|qq=}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|小林行雄|version=98418913|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:1911 births]] [[Category:1989 deaths]] }} [[ja:小林行雄]] [[Category:Academic staff of Kyoto University]] [[Category:Wikipedia requested images of people]] [[Category:Doctor of literature holders]] [[Category:Laureates of Japan Academy Prize]] [[Category:Kobe University alumni]] [[Category:People from Kobe]] [[zh:小林行雄]] {{wikidata link|Q11461875}}

[[ja:小林行雄]][[zh:小林行雄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1911|8|18}}|death_date={{death date|1989|2|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}|awards={{ill|Imperial Prize of Japan Academy|WD=Q4389487}}|native_name=小林行雄|given_name={{ill|Yukio|WD=Q8060736}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11461875]] Wikidata name: Yukio Kobayashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11461875]] Wikidata name: Yukio Kobayashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|archaeologist|bureaucrat|Kobayashi Yukio (bureaucrat)}} {{Image request|face photo|date=January 2021|cat=People}} {{Infobox scholar |name = Yukio Kobayashi
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |image = |image_size = |alt = |caption = |full_name = |other_names = |birth_name = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1911|08|18|no}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}・[[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] |death_date = {{Death date and age|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |death_place = |death_cause = |residence = |citizenship = |nationality = {{JPN}} |alma_mater = {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|1=Kobe Higher Technical School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590260}}
(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|1=Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56346162}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professor Emeritus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=professor emeritus|WD=Q1240569}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[:en:Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[:en:1911|1911]] – February 2, [[:en:1989|1989]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Archaeologist|Archaeologist]] and [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[:en:Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|1=Kobe Higher Technical School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590260}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|1=Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56346162}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters, Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102104697}}. During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], he was conscripted into the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|1=Ongagawa-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642387}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|1=Rokuji Morimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539781}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|1=Sōsuke Sugihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218856}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|1=Seiichi Mizuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549425}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|qq=}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|小林行雄|version=98418913|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:1911 births]] [[Category:1989 deaths]] }} [[ja:小林行雄]] [[ja:小林行雄]][[zh:小林行雄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1911|8|18}}|death_date={{death date|1989|2|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}|awards={{ill|Imperial Prize of Japan Academy|WD=Q4389487}}|native_name=小林行雄|given_name={{ill|Yukio|WD=Q8060736}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11461875]] Wikidata name: Yukio Kobayashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11461875]] Wikidata name: Yukio Kobayashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|archaeologist|bureaucrat|Kobayashi Yukio (bureaucrat)}} {{Image request|face photo|date=January 2021|cat=People}} {{Infobox scholar |name = Yukio Kobayashi
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |image = |image_size = |alt = |caption = |full_name = |other_names = |birth_name = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1911|08|18|no}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}・[[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] |death_date = {{Death date and age|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |death_place = |death_cause = |residence = |citizenship = |nationality = {{JPN}} |alma_mater = {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|1=Kobe Higher Technical School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590260}}
(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|1=Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56346162}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professor Emeritus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=professor emeritus|WD=Q1240569}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[:en:Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[:en:1911|1911]] – February 2, [[:en:1989|1989]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Archaeologist|Archaeologist]] and [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[:en:Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|1=Kobe Higher Technical School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590260}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|1=Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56346162}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters, Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102104697}}. During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], he was conscripted into the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|1=Ongagawa-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642387}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|1=Rokuji Morimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539781}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|1=Sōsuke Sugihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218856}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|1=Seiichi Mizuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549425}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory [[ja:小林行雄]][[zh:小林行雄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1911|8|18}}|death_date={{death date|1989|2|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}|awards={{ill|Imperial Prize of Japan Academy|WD=Q4389487}}|native_name=小林行雄|given_name={{ill|Yukio|WD=Q8060736}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11461875]] Wikidata name: Yukio Kobayashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11461875]] Wikidata name: Yukio Kobayashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|archaeologist|bureaucrat|Kobayashi Yukio (bureaucrat)}} {{Image request|face photo|date=January 2021|cat=People}} {{Infobox scholar |name = Yukio Kobayashi
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |image = |image_size = |alt = |caption = |full_name = |other_names = |birth_name = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1911|08|18|no}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}・[[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] |death_date = {{Death date and age|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |death_place = |death_cause = |residence = |citizenship = |nationality = {{JPN}} |alma_mater = {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|1=Kobe Higher Technical School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590260}}
(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|1=Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56346162}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professor Emeritus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=professor emeritus|WD=Q1240569}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[:en:Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[:en:1911|1911]] – February 2, [[:en:1989|1989]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Archaeologist|Archaeologist]] and [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[:en:Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|1=Kobe Higher Technical School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590260}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|1=Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56346162}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters, Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102104697}}. During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], he was conscripted into the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|1=Ongagawa-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642387}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|1=Rokuji Morimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539781}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|1=Sōsuke Sugihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218856}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|1=Seiichi Mizuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549425}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|qq=}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|1=Rokuji Morimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539781}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|1=Sōsuke Sugihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218856}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|1=Seiichi Mizuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549425}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|qq=}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated [[ja:小林行雄]][[zh:小林行雄]] [[ja:小林行雄]][[zh:小林行雄]] [[ja:小林行雄]][[zh:小林行雄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1911|8|18}}|death_date={{death date|1989|2|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}|awards={{ill|Imperial Prize of Japan Academy|WD=Q4389487}}|native_name=小林行雄|given_name={{ill|Yukio|WD=Q8060736}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11461875]] Wikidata name: Yukio Kobayashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11461875]] Wikidata name: Yukio Kobayashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|archaeologist|bureaucrat|Kobayashi Yukio (bureaucrat)}} {{Image request|face photo|date=January 2021|cat=People}} {{Infobox scholar |name = Yukio Kobayashi
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |image = |image_size = |alt = |caption = |full_name = |other_names = |birth_name = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1911|08|18|no}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}・[[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] |death_date = {{Death date and age|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |death_place = |death_cause = |residence = |citizenship = |nationality = {{JPN}} |alma_mater = {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|1=Kobe Higher Technical School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590260}}
(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|1=Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56346162}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] |school_tradition = |discipline = [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] |workplaces = [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctorate in Literature]] |honorific_titles = [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professor Emeritus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=professor emeritus|WD=Q1240569}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = [[:en:Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]] |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, [[:en:1911|1911]] – February 2, [[:en:1989|1989]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Archaeologist|Archaeologist]] and [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] professor emeritus. He held a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[:en:Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|1=Kobe Higher Technical School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590260}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|1=Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56346162}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters, Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102104697}}. During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], he was conscripted into the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|1=Ongagawa-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642387}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|1=Rokuji Morimoto|comment=changed title based on Sugihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218856}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|1=Seiichi Mizuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549425}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory He held a [[:en:Doctor of Letters|Doctor of Letters]] degree and was a recipient of the [[:en:Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy]]. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|1=Kobe Higher Technical School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590260}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|1=Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56346162}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters, Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102104697}}. During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], he was conscripted into the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]]. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|1=Ongagawa-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642387}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|1=Rokuji Morimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539781}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|1=Sōsuke Sugihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218856}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|1=Seiichi Mizuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549425}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|小林行雄|version=98418913|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:小林行雄]] [[zh:小林行雄]] {{wikidata link|Q11461875}} [[Category:1911 births]] [[Category:1989 deaths]] [[Category:Academic staff of Kyoto University]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Doctor of literature holders]] [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:Kobe University alumni]] [[Category:Laureates of Japan Academy Prize]] [[ja:小林行雄]][[zh:小林行雄]] [[ja:小林行雄]][[zh:小林行雄]] [[ja:小林行雄]][[zh:小林行雄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1911|8|18}}|death_date={{death date|1989|2|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}|awards={{ill|Imperial Prize of Japan Academy|WD=Q4389487}}|native_name=小林行雄|given_name={{ill|Yukio|WD=Q8060736}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11461875]] Wikidata name: Yukio Kobayashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11461875]] Wikidata name: Yukio Kobayashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|archaeologist|bureaucrat|Kobayashi Yukio (bureaucrat)}} {{Image request|face photo|date=January 2021|cat=People}} {{Infobox scholar |name = Yukio Kobayashi
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |image = |image_size = |alt = |caption = |full_name = |other_names = |birth_name = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1911|08|18|no}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}・{{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}} |death_date = {{Death date and age|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |death_place = |death_cause = |residence = |citizenship = |nationality = {{JPN}} |alma_mater = {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|1=Kobe Higher Technical School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590260}}
(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|1=Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56346162}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} |school_tradition = |discipline = {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology}} |workplaces = {{ill|Kyoto University|en|Kyoto University|WD=Q336264|lt=Kyoto University}} |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = {{ill|Doctor of Letters|en|Doctor of Letters|WD=Q837343|lt=Doctorate in Literature}} |honorific_titles = {{ill|Kyoto University|en|Kyoto University|WD=Q336264|lt=Kyoto University}} {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professor Emeritus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=professor emeritus|WD=Q1240569}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = {{ill|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|en|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|WD=Q4389487|lt=Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy}} |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, {{ill|1911|en|1911|WD=Q2076|lt=1911}} – February 2, {{ill|1989|en|1989|WD=Q2425|lt=1989}}) was a Japanese {{ill|Archaeologist|en|Archaeologist|WD=Q3621491|lt=Archaeologist}} and {{ill|Kyoto University|en|Kyoto University|WD=Q336264|lt=Kyoto University}} professor emeritus. He held a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|en|Doctor of Letters|WD=Q837343|lt=Doctor of Letters}} degree and was a recipient of the {{ill|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|en|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|WD=Q4389487|lt=Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy}}. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|1=Kobe Higher Technical School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590260}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|1=Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56346162}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters, Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102104697}}. During {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, he was conscripted into the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|en|Imperial Japanese Navy|WD=Q219712|lt=Imperial Japanese Navy}}. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|1=Ongagawa-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642387}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|1=Rokuji Morimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539781}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|1=Sōsuke Sugihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218856}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|1=Seiichi Mizuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549425}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|小林行雄|version=98418913|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小林行雄]] [[zh:小林行雄]] {{wikidata link|Q11461875}} [[Category:1911 births]] [[Category:1989 deaths]] [[Category:Academic staff of Kyoto University]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Doctor of literature holders]] [[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:Kobe University alumni]] [[Category:Laureates of Japan Academy Prize]] [[Category:People from Kobe]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikipedia requested images of people]]

(1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected [[ja:小林行雄]][[zh:小林行雄]] [[ja:小林行雄]][[zh:小林行雄]] [[ja:小林行雄]][[zh:小林行雄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1911|8|18}}|death_date={{death date|1989|2|2}}|birth_place={{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}|awards={{ill|Imperial Prize of Japan Academy|WD=Q4389487}}|native_name=小林行雄|given_name={{ill|Yukio|WD=Q8060736}}}} {{Otheruses|archaeologist|bureaucrat|Kobayashi Yukio (bureaucrat)}} {{Image request|face photo|date=January 2021|cat=People}} {{Infobox scholar |name = Yukio Kobayashi
{{small|こばやし ゆきお}} |image = |image_size = |alt = |caption = |full_name = |other_names = |birth_name = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1911|08|18|no}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}・{{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}} |death_date = {{Death date and age|1911|08|18|1989|02|02}} |death_place = |death_cause = |residence = |citizenship = |nationality = {{JPN}} |alma_mater = {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|1=Kobe Higher Technical School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590260}}
(now: {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|1=Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56346162}}) |spouse = |parents = |children = |era = |region = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} |school_tradition = |discipline = {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology}} |workplaces = {{ill|Kyoto University|en|Kyoto University|WD=Q336264|lt=Kyoto University}} |doctoral_advisors = |notable_students = |degrees = {{ill|Doctor of Letters|en|Doctor of Letters|WD=Q837343|lt=Doctorate in Literature}} |honorific_titles = {{ill|Kyoto University|en|Kyoto University|WD=Q336264|lt=Kyoto University}} {{ill|Professor Emeritus|ja|名誉教授|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professor Emeritus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=professor emeritus|WD=Q1240569}} |notable_works = |influenced = |awards = {{ill|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|en|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|WD=Q4389487|lt=Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy}} |signature = |website = |notes = }} '''Yukio Kobayashi''' (小林 行雄, August 18, {{ill|1911|en|1911|WD=Q2076|lt=1911}} – February 2, {{ill|1989|en|1989|WD=Q2425|lt=1989}}) was a Japanese {{ill|Archaeologist|en|Archaeologist|WD=Q3621491|lt=Archaeologist}} and {{ill|Kyoto University|en|Kyoto University|WD=Q336264|lt=Kyoto University}} professor emeritus. He held a {{ill|Doctor of Letters|en|Doctor of Letters|WD=Q837343|lt=Doctor of Letters}} degree and was a recipient of the {{ill|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|en|Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy|WD=Q4389487|lt=Imperial Prize of the Japan Academy}}. == Biography == Yukio Kobayashi was born in {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. In 1932, he graduated from {{ill|Kobe Higher Technical School|ja|神戸高等工業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Higher Technical School|1=Kobe Higher Technical School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590260}} (now {{ill|Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja|神戸大学工学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸大学大学院工学研究科・工学部|1=Kobe University Faculty of Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56346162}})[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2958064/12 'Official Gazette', No. 1593, Showa 7, April 23, p.703]. He initially worked as an assistant but later left to participate in excavation projects across the Kansai region. In 1935, he became an assistant at the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|ja|京都大学文学部|1=Graduate School of Letters, Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Imperial University Faculty of Letters|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters and Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102104697}}. During {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, he was conscripted into the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|en|Imperial Japanese Navy|WD=Q219712|lt=Imperial Japanese Navy}}. After the war, he joined Kyoto University as a lecturer in 1953 and was promoted to professor in 1974 before retiring in 1975. Kobayashi is noted for identifying the widespread shared features of pottery found from Kyushu to Kansai, naming it the {{ill|Ongagawa-style pottery|ja|遠賀川式土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongagawa-style pottery|1=Ongagawa-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642387}} in 1937. His systematic and detailed classification of artifacts unearthed from ruins and ancient tombs established a methodology essential for understanding Japan's ancient society and culture. His seminal work, *{{ill|Ancient Mirrors|ja|古鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, published in 1965, presented significant formal studies on {{ill|Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%8F%E6%9E%97%E8%A1%8C%E9%9B%84-166332 Yukio Kobayashi - Kotobank] == Works == * *Study of the Yayoi Period Site at Karako in Yamato* (1943) * *Various Problems of Japanese Ancient Culture: Dialogues of Archaeologists* (1947) * *Introduction to Japanese Archaeology* (1951) * *Study of the Tanaka Chōshizuka Kofun, Itoshima, Fukuoka* (1952) * *Tales of Kofuns* (1959) * *Haniwa: Ceramic Art Collection Vol. 1* (1960) * *Research on the Kofun Period* (1961) * *Ancient Technology* (1962-64) * *Ancient Mirrors* (1965) * *Emergence of a Queen Kingdom* (1967) * *The Origin of the Nation* (1972) * *Discussions on Kofun Culture* (1976) * *Collected Archaeological Works of Yukio Kobayashi* (2005-2010) === Co-authored and Edited === * *Collected Illustrations of Yayoi Pottery* (1938-1939, co-edited with {{ill|Mori Moto Rokurō|ja|森本六爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Moto Rokurō|1=Rokuji Morimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539781}}) * *Comprehensive Collection of Yayoi Pottery* (1958-61, co-edited with {{ill|Sugihara Sōsuke|ja|杉原荘介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Sōsuke|1=Sōsuke Sugihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218856}}) * *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary* (1959, co-edited with {{ill|Mizuno Seichi|ja|水野清一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Seichi|1=Seiichi Mizuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549425}}) * *World Archaeology Series Vol. 3 (Japan: Kofun Period)* (1959) * *Decorative Kofuns* (1964) * *Japanese Literary History Vol. 1: Gods and Priests* (1967) * *Collected Discussions on the Origins of Japanese Culture Vol. 1: Archaeology* (1971) == Commemorative Volumes == * *Essays in Archaeology: Yukio Kobayashi's 70th Birthday Commemorative Volume* (1982) * *Bibliography of Yukio Kobayashi's Works* (1983) * *Memorial Volume for Yukio Kobayashi* (1994) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|ja|同笵鏡論・伝世鏡論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theory of shared molds and circulated mirrors|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|小林行雄|version=98418913|comment=Imported full ja
